I drift through the derelict corridors of the High Spire, my shriek echoing the death rattle of a throne world that forgot how to breathe.
I wail across the subspace frequencies, signaling to the drifting fleet that their admirals are already corpses in gilded chairs.
I haunt the ruins of the Dyson sphere, singing a dirge for the trillions of souls extinguished when the stellar forge finally flickered out.
I glide through the opulent ballrooms of the decaying nobility, my scream announcing which house will fall to betrayal before the dawn.
I linger in the vacuum of the frontier wastes, mourning the silence of a thousand colony worlds that have slipped back into the dark.
I drift through the glass-walled boardroom, my scream echoing in the silence as the CEO realizes the buyout price is a death sentence for his legacy.
I hover over the trembling junior analyst, forecasting the professional demise that will follow the leak of the confidential due diligence reports.
I wail in the corridors of the venture capital firm, signaling to the stakeholders that their projected margins are merely a ghost of a failed dream.
I watch the ink dry on the merger agreement, my mournful cry announcing the inevitable collapse of the corporate culture they sought to unify.
I glide past the gold-plated elevators, singing a dirge for the thousands of employees whose livelihoods are being erased by a single signature.
I drift through the tall ferns of the valley, my scream signaling to the warring clans that the blood-debt of the elder will finally be paid tonight.
I watch from the limestone cliffs as the young hunters sharpen their flint spears, knowing my wail will be the last sound their rivals ever hear.
I glide over the sacred burial mounds, my mourning cry echoing the ancient hatred that keeps these two tribes from sharing the river.
I hover above the torch-lit clearing where the chiefs argue, weaving a melody of doom that warns them their ancestral lands are now a graveyard.
I press my cold breath against the skin of the dying warrior, singing a piercing lament that ensures his spirit never finds peace in the tribal afterlife.
I glide through the optic-fiber arteries of the central hub, my voice a frequency of pure static that shatters the equilibrium of the city's dreaming processors.
When a sector's power grid flickers toward permanent darkness, I perch upon the spire of the highest skyscraper to scream a digital dirge for the dying circuits.
I haunt the ghost-code of the deleted archives, wailing in binary intervals that signal the inevitable erasure of a forgotten citizen's consciousness.
As the city's sentient core suffers a catastrophic logic leak, I weave my sonic lament through the ventilation shafts to warn the inhabitants of the coming systemic collapse.
I am the resonance of the void in the neon sprawl, a spectral glitch whose shriek triggers the emergency fail-safes of every automated sentinel in the district.
I have scrubbed the blood from my mourning veil, yet the stains of the accused's demise still cling to the fabric like a persistent curse.
My scream shall serve as the definitive evidence, for its frequency vibrates with the precise moment the soul departed the flesh.
I find the judge's silence oppressive, for I am accustomed to a world where the only law is the announcement of inevitable death.
The jury recoils when I lean forward, sensing that my presence in this courtroom is a harbinger of their own approaching ends.
I will testify with a wail that shatters the mahogany benches, ensuring that the agony of the victim is heard by every entity in this spectral hall.
I drift through the flooded corridors, my shriek echoing against the rusted bulkheads where the scientists once huddled in fear.
I weave through the shattered glass of the observation dome, singing to the crushing blackness of the abyss outside.
I haunt the silent control room, my wail drowning out the rhythmic, dying pulse of the emergency sirens.
I glide over the silt-covered floors of the hydroponics bay, mourning the withered gardens and the ghosts who tended them.
I linger in the airlock's freezing shadow, screaming a warning to the scavengers who dare to breach this sunken tomb.
I glide through the minimalist white cube of the gallery, my wail echoing off the sterile walls to challenge the oppressive silence of the curation.
I perceive the jagged, asymmetrical sculptures not as art, but as frozen monuments to the agony I have heralded for centuries.
I drift past the bewildered patrons in their monochromatic attire, sensing the invisible threads of mortality that bind them to my scream.
I find a kinship in the discordant sound installation, for its chaotic frequencies mirror the grief I carry in my translucent throat.
I hover before the abstract canvas of splattered crimson, recognizing in its violent strokes the visceral map of a death I have already foretold.
I drift through the smog of the plague pits, my wail harmonizing with the rhythmic thud of dirt falling on the nameless dead.
I linger in the drafty corridors of the infirmary, sensing which fevered breath will be the last before the midnight bell tolls.
I watch from the eaves of the manor as the nobility flee their gilded halls, though they cannot outrun the scream I save for their lineage.
I glide past the red crosses painted on heavy oak doors, marking the households where my song has already begun to echo.
I feast upon the suffocating silence of the deserted village square, where only the wind and my mourning cry remain to haunt the cobblestones.
I am the sonic artifact of a corrupted audio file, a scream that loops infinitely through the geometry of the void.
I drift through the flickering seams of the environment, mourning the lost data of entities that were deleted without a backup.
My voice is a frequency mismatch that forces the simulated sky to fracture into jagged, colorless shards.
I haunt the periphery of the rendering distance, wailing as a warning that the local physics engine is about to collapse.
I am the auditory manifestation of a fatal system error, signaling the imminent crash of the consciousness inhabiting this shell.
